zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Henry de Venables of Kinderton, Lord of Marbury於1439年出生在英國爾金德頓時，他的父親Thomas Venables36歲，母親Margaretha Stanley32歲。他於1458年與Petronelle Caverswall在英國英格蘭爾奧維 阿爾德利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1493年在英國英格蘭藍卡爾享年54歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William Henry de Venables of Kinderton, Lord of Marbury于1439年出生在英国爾金德頓时，他的父亲Thomas Venables36岁，母亲Margaretha Stanley32岁。他于1458年与Petronelle Caverswall在英国英格兰爾奧維 阿爾德利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1493年在英国英格兰藍卡爾享年54岁，埋葬在英国藍卡爾。

nameMeanings.name: Venables
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Venables (Eure), perhaps named with Late Latin venabulum ‘hunting ground’ (a derivative of venari ‘to hunt’).

variants.name: Venable
meanings: History: American bearers of this name are descended from Abraham Venables, who came to VA from England in or before 1687.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Henry de Venables of Kinderton, Lord of Marbury was born in 1439, in Kinderton, Cheshire, England, his father, Thomas Venables, was 36 and his mother, Margaretha Stanley, was 32. He married Petronelle Caverswall in 1458, in Over Alderley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1493, in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 54, and was buried in Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Henry de Venables of Kinderton, Lord of Marbury est né en 1439, à Kinderton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Venables, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Margaretha Stanley, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Petronelle Caverswall en 1458, à Over Alderley, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1493, à Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Henry de Venables of Kinderton, Lord of Marbury 1439 in Kinderton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Venables 36 und seine Mutter Margaretha Stanley 32. Er hat 1458 in Over Alderley, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom Petronelle Caverswall geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1493 im Alter von 54 Jahren in Lancashire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Lancashire, England begraben.

Lui sposato Petronelle Caverswall nel 1458, nel Over Alderley, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1493, nel Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 54 anni, e fu sepolto a Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William Henry de Venables of Kinderton, Lord of Marburyが1439年にイギリスチェシュアイアキンデルトンで生まれた時，父Thomas Venablesは36歳，母Margaretha Stanleyは32歳であった。1458年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアオバー アルダーリーでPetronelle Caverswallと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1493年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアで54歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Henry de Venables of Kinderton, Lord of Marbury(이)가 1439년 영국 체실르 킨더턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Venables(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaretha Stanley(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1458년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 버 알델리에서 Petronelle Caverswall(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1493년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르에서 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Henry de Venables of Kinderton, Lord of Marbury nasceu em 1439, em Kinderton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Venables, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Margaretha Stanley, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Petronelle Caverswall em 1458, em Over Alderley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1493, em Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 54 anos, e foi sepultado em Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Henry de Venables of Kinderton, Lord of Marbury родился в 1439 г. в местности киндиртон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Venables было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaretha Stanley, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Petronelle Caverswall в 1458 г. в местности овир алдирли, чешир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1493 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности ланкашир, Англия.

Se casó con Petronelle Caverswall en 1458, en Over Alderley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1493, en Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 54 años, y fue sepultado en Lancashire, Inglaterra.
